2606.13668.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
2606.13668.paper_summary: With the growth of LLMs' (Large Language Models) capabilities, there has been an increasing push to curate high quality datasets by filtering samples in the training data. In general, Data Attribution (DA) methods aim to estimate how individual samples in a training dataset can precondition a model to generate certain outputs. As an example, one might be interested in which samples in the data could be the source of toxic behavior after training the LLM. Many methods quantify this conditioning through the paradigm of influence functions. While methods of this family are effective in its function, they lack the necessary processing speed and storage compactness to be practically implemented on large datasets. We propose a method, Influcoder, as a quick and cost-effective approach to influence-based Data Attribution at scale.
2606.13668.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）能力的增長，對於通過過濾訓練數據中的樣本來策劃高質量數據集的推動也在不斷增加。<lb/>一般來說，數據歸因（DA）方法旨在估計訓練數據集中個別樣本如何使模型預先條件化以生成某些輸出。<lb/>例如，人們可能會對哪些數據樣本在訓練LLM後可能成為有毒行為的來源感興趣。<lb/>許多方法通過影響函數的範式來量化這種條件化。<lb/>雖然這類方法在功能上是有效的，但它們缺乏必要的處理速度和存儲緊湊性，無法在大型數據集上實際應用。<lb/>我們提出了一種方法，Influcoder，作為一種快速且具成本效益的影響基數據歸因方法，適用於大規模應用。
2606.13668.author: Dimitri Kachler et.al.
2606.13668.authors: Dimitri Kachler, Damien Sileo, Pascal Denis
